steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Words in Context. The decisive clue is "These characteristic works" in the next sentence — the examples are described as defining and "esteemed," so the blank must express that Edwards is widely known for portrait painting, not that he dislikes, doubts, or is obligated to do it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘complaint about’: means dissatisfaction or grievance — contradicts the passage’s positive framing of his "esteemed" characteristic works. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘reputation for’: means widely recognized for an activity — fits the context of celebrated, characteristic portraits that define his career. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘question about’: means uncertainty or doubt — the text presents his portrait work as an established, defining practice, not something he is uncertain about. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘requirement for’: means mandatory obligation — the text shows portrait painting as a chosen, celebrated hallmark of his art, not an externally imposed duty. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
